simulator.hero_title: Which cyber regulations apply to you?
simulator.hero_text: Answer a few questions and immediately discover which frameworks are applicable or recommended for your organisation.
simulator.organization_subtitle: These 6 questions determine the applicable regulations
simulator.q1_label: Where is your registered office / main activity?*
simulator.q1_option_eu_clients: Outside EU but clients/users in the EU
simulator.q2_option_ict_b2b: ICT B2B services (MSP, MSSP, managed services)
simulator.q2_option_digital_providers: Digital providers (marketplaces, search engines, social networks)
simulator.q3_option_small: Small: fewer than 50 employees AND turnover <€10M AND balance sheet <€10M
simulator.q3_option_medium: Medium: 50-249 employees OR turnover €10-50M OR balance sheet €10-43M
simulator.q3_option_large: Large: 250+ employees OR turnover >€50M OR balance sheet >€43M
simulator.disclaimer: This simulator provides a general indication and does not constitute legal advice.
simulator.q2_label: What is your main business sector?*
simulator.q3_label: What is the size of your organisation?*
simulator.q3_hint: NIS 2: <50 employees AND <€10M = not in scope. Any single threshold exceeded suffices.
simulator.q4_label: Do you process personal data?*
simulator.q4_hint: Name, email, phone, IP, cookies, HR data, customer files... 95% of companies = Yes
simulator.q5_label: Do you hold a financial licence (AMF, ACPR, CSSF...)?*
simulator.q5_hint: DORA targets exclusively regulated financial entities. The 'banking/finance' sector alone is NOT sufficient without a licence!
simulator.q6_label: Do you provide IT/Cloud services to clients?*
simulator.q6_hint: MSP, managed services, SaaS, hosting, IT consulting...
simulator.conditional_subtitle: These questions refine your diagnosis based on your profile
simulator.q7_label: Are you an ICT service provider for regulated financial entities?
simulator.q7_hint: Critical IT subcontractor for banks, insurers, asset managers... → DORA applies
simulator.q8_label: Do you host health data for third parties (clients)?
simulator.q8_hint: For clients (hospitals, doctors, clinics). NOT your own internal data.
simulator.q9_label: Do you store/process bank card numbers (PAN)?
simulator.q9_hint: NOT applicable if you use Stripe/PayPal without ever seeing the numbers.
simulator.q10_label: Do you have activities or clients in the United States?*
simulator.q10a_label: Do your US clients require certifications (ISO 27001 / SOC 2)?
simulator.q10b_label: Do you work with the US Department of Defense (DoD)?
simulator.q11_label: Do you have critical activities requiring a business continuity plan?*
simulator.continuity_note: NIS 2 / DORA requires business continuity measures (Art. 21). A plan will be required even if you don't have one yet.
simulator.no_results_text: Based on your answers, we have not identified any mandatory regulation.
simulator.nis2_extraterritorial_note: Sectors subject to NIS 2 extra-territorial effect: (1) Digital infrastructure (IXP, DNS, TLD, datacentres), (2) ICT B2B services (MSP, MSSP, managed services), (3) Digital providers (marketplaces, search engines, social networks).
simulator.pdf_text: Get a PDF summary with all regulations, risks and deadlines.
simulator.pdf_popup_blocked: Popup blocked. Please allow popups for this site.
simulator.cta_text: Get a personalised audit and a compliance roadmap.

rgpd.sanctions: Up to €20M or 4% of global annual turnover

nis2.sanctions: Essential Entities: up to €10M or 2% turnover | Important Entities: up to €7M or 1.4% turnover

pcidss.fullName: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
pcidss.sanctions: $5K-$100K/month depending on duration + card processing withdrawal

iso27001.sanctions: Loss of contracts, exclusion from tenders

iso22301.sanctions: Risk of extended outage, financial losses

cmmc.sanctions: Exclusion from DoD contracts + False Claims Act fines

why.rgpd: Processing personal data with activity in the EU or EU clients/users
why.dora_regulated: EU financial entity holding a licence (AMF, ACPR, CSSF or equivalent)
why.dora_tic: Critical ICT service provider supplying EU regulated financial entities
why.nis2_eu_annex1_ee: Annex I sector (highly critical) + Sufficient size + EU registered → Essential Entity
why.nis2_eu_annex1_ei: Annex I sector (highly critical) + Sufficient size + EU registered → Important Entity
why.nis2_eu_annex2_ee: Annex II sector (critical) + Sufficient size + EU registered → Essential Entity
why.nis2_eu_annex2_ei: Annex II sector (critical) + Sufficient size + EU registered → Important Entity
why.nis2_extraterritorial: NIS 2 extra-territorial effect (Art. 26): entity outside EU providing services to EU clients
why.pcidss: Storage, processing or transmission of bank card numbers (PAN)
why.hds: Hosting health personal data on behalf of third parties
why.iso27001_mandatory: Certification contractually required by your clients
why.iso27001_recommended: International reference standard for information security
why.soc2_mandatory: Attestation required by your US clients
why.soc2_recommended: Standard expected by US clients for SaaS/Cloud providers
why.iso22301_dora_nis2: DORA + NIS 2 regulatory requirement (Art. 21) — business continuity plan mandatory
why.iso22301_dora: DORA regulatory requirement — business continuity plan mandatory
why.iso22301_nis2: NIS 2 regulatory requirement (Art. 21) — business continuity plan mandatory
why.iso22301_critical_sector: Critical sector — business continuity plan strongly recommended
why.iso22301_critical_activity: Critical activities identified — business continuity plan recommended
why.cmmc: Direct contracts or subcontracting with the US Department of Defense (DoD)

simulator.pdf_audit_section_text: To implement the standards identified in this diagnosis, I offer personalised audits tailored to your context. Contact me at the address below to discuss your needs.

grc.title: GRC Cybersecurity — AgileVizion
grc.hero_h1: 10 million euros in fines or 2 % of global revenue — and that's just NIS 2
grc.hero_subtitle: DORA (Digital Operational Resilience Act), NIS 2 (Network and Information Security Directive 2), ISO 27001, GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) — every month a new requirement, every breach a penalty. I guide you from audit to compliance.
grc.problems_title: Are you asking yourself these questions?
grc.problem_1_text: DORA, NIS 2, GDPR, ISO 27001, CIS Controls... The proliferation of frameworks makes it difficult to identify your actual obligations — and those that don't concern you.
grc.problem_2_text: Some regulations expose you to severe financial and criminal penalties. Others are simply best practices. Confusing the two is costly — either through over-investment or fines.
grc.problem_3_text: The fines set by EU regulations are massive:
grc.problem_3_nis2: up to €10 M or 2 % of global annual turnover
grc.problem_3_rgpd: up to €20 M or 4 % of global annual turnover
grc.problem_4_title: What is the risk for executives?
grc.problem_4_text: DORA and NIS 2 introduce personal liability for executives: individual fines, mandatory cyber-risk training obligations, and possible dismissal in case of serious breach.
grc.solution_title: The solution: structured audit and compliance
grc.solution_text: I transform regulatory complexity into a clear, prioritised and actionable plan — without mobilising your teams for months.
grc.solution_point1: Precise identification of your actual regulatory obligations
grc.solution_point2: Methodical field audit with evidence collection and professional deliverables
grc.solution_point3: Prioritised roadmap by criticality, effort and impact
grc.approach_1_text: Assessment of the applicable regulatory scope: identification of mandatory standards, size thresholds, relevant sectors, and compliance deadlines.
grc.approach_2_text: Documentary and on-site controls, stakeholder interviews, evidence collection. Structured grid of 100 to 160+ control points per standard.
grc.approach_3_text: Action plan prioritised by criticality, effort and regulatory deadline. Each gap is documented with a concrete recommendation and a designated owner.
grc.deliverable_1_text: 100 to 160+ control points per standard, with status (compliant / non-compliant / partial) and supporting evidence.
grc.deliverable_2_text: Management summary with key indicators + technical detail usable by security teams.
grc.deliverable_3_text: Actions ranked by criticality, estimated effort and regulatory deadline. Each action is traceable to a gap.
grc.deliverable_4_text: Formal closing document attesting to the completion of the audit, signed by all parties.
grc.deliverable_5_text: Ready-to-use templates: ISMS (Information Security Management System) policy, incident procedures, DPIA (Data Protection Impact Assessment).
grc.deliverable_6_text: Visual overview of the framework and your compliance level — shareable with management and stakeholders.
grc.frameworks_subtitle: Complete audit kits, tested and ready to use
grc.cta_title: Ready to gain clarity on your compliance?

ia.title: Agentic AI — AgileVizion
ia.hero_h1: Your teams are wasting time on tasks AI already knows how to do
ia.hero_subtitle: A dedicated AI agent prepares, produces, verifies and learns — so your employees can focus on what truly matters.
ia.problems_subtitle: The research is consistent: your teams' cognitive time is wasted on mechanical tasks.
ia.kpi_p1_label: of working time spent on repetitive, low-value tasks
ia.kpi_p2_label: of employees lack time to do the strategic work expected of them
ia.kpi_p3_label: more errors made under pressure and cognitive overload
ia.consequence_2: Costly errors and omissions in document workflows
ia.consequence_3: Talent under-utilised, frustrated by mechanical tasks
ia.consequence_4: Growth capacity limited without additional headcount
ia.augmented_title: The augmented employee with a dedicated AI agent
ia.augmented_subtitle: An AI agent is an autonomous programme that completes complex tasks across multiple steps, using tools and making decisions — without human intervention at every stage.
ia.verb_1_desc: Searches, collects and structures the information you need, before you even ask for it
ia.verb_2_desc: Generates structured deliverables — reports, analyses, documents — based on collected data
ia.verb_3_desc: Checks quality, detects inconsistencies and validates against defined criteria — zero omissions
ia.verb_4_desc: Improves with each iteration by integrating your feedback and new business requirements
ia.augmented_key_message: The employee remains in control — the agent amplifies their work, it does not replace them.
ia.example_subtitle: An AI agent works in 2 coordinated phases to produce a complete, traceable compliance audit.
ia.phase_1_item_1: Searching and downloading regulatory texts (DORA, NIS 2, ISO 27001…)
ia.phase_1_item_3: Verification against 7 quality criteria: completeness, accuracy, traceability, relevance, currency, format, usability
ia.phase_1_item_4: Automatic structuring into a knowledge base
ia.phase_2_item_1: Automatic pre-filling of the audit grid (100 to 160+ control points)
ia.phase_2_item_2: Generation of executive and technical reports
ia.phase_2_item_4: The consultant reviews, adjusts and signs — expert judgement is preserved
ia.jobs_subtitle: Agentic AI is not reserved for IT teams — every function can save hours every week.
ia.job_1_item_1: Automatic collection of standards and regulatory updates
ia.job_2_item_1: Automated code review and vulnerability detection
ia.job_3_item_1: Legal monitoring and analysis of legislative texts
ia.job_3_item_3: Case summaries and case law research
ia.job_4_item_1: Automated extraction and reconciliation of financial data
ia.job_5_item_1: Screening and analysis of applications against defined criteria
ia.job_5_item_2: Writing job descriptions and job postings
ia.job_5_item_3: Automated tracking of legal HR obligations
ia.job_7_item_1: Automated weekly KPI summary and alerts
ia.job_7_item_2: Preparation of decision notes and presentations
ia.approach_1_text: 1 to 2 days to map your processes and identify high-ROI (Return on Investment) automation opportunities.
ia.approach_2_text: 1 to 3 weeks to develop and configure AI agents adapted to your tools, data and business constraints.
ia.approach_3_text: Team training, performance monitoring, iterative adjustments. The agent improves with your feedback.
ia.results_subtitle: Observed on agentic AI projects deployed in real-world contexts
ia.result_kpi_2_label: of time spent on document production
ia.result_kpi_3_label: omissions — quality control is systematic and traceable
ia.cta_title: Ready to identify what AI can do for you?
ia.cta_subtitle: 30-minute diagnostic — free, no commitment. We identify together the 2-3 most automatable tasks in your context.
